intended_uses_covered: Separate independent living quarters for one family
possible_placement_paths: Possible path: Movable Tiny House as an ADU under Los Angeles ADU/MTH rules, if the parcel, primary dwelling context, structure, utilities, and permit application satisfy current city requirements.
clearly_allowed: Los Angeles official materials include Movable Tiny Houses as one ADU type and provide permit/design guidance for using an MTH as an ADU.
The LADBS ADU page says the ADU ordinance includes ADUs, JADUs, and Movable Tiny Houses under the cited local and state code framework.
unclear: Whether a specific parcel is eligible.
Which zoning overlays, hillside/coastal/fire/access constraints, utility requirements, or site-specific limits apply.
Whether a specific tiny home design meets all MTH requirements.
Whether HOA, deed, lender, or insurance constraints apply.
local_office_to_contact: Los Angeles Department of Building and Safety (LADBS)
source_quote_or_summary: LADBS says the Los Angeles ADU ordinance defines ADUs, JADUs, and Movable Tiny Houses as ADU types under the local ordinance and state code framework.

The LADBS information bulletin is specifically for guidelines on using and permitting a Movable Tiny House as an ADU and defines an MTH as separate independent living quarters for one family subject to listed requirements.
Los Angeles City Planning's housing policy page is a supporting source for housing policy context, but parcel-specific zoning still requires city verification.
notes: High confidence for the existence of an official Los Angeles MTH-as-ADU path.
Do not imply blanket approval. This record should only surface as a possible path with parcel and permit verification.

intended_uses_covered: Accessory dwelling unit / companion unit path
Residential use subject to ADU-related standards
possible_placement_paths: Possible path: Movable Tiny House under San Diego's ADU / companion unit-related framework, if the parcel, zone, structure, access, utilities, coastal/fire/hazard constraints, and permits satisfy current city requirements.
clearly_allowed: San Diego's adopted code update page says the City Council adopted Movable Tiny Homes as a separately regulated residential use within existing companion unit and junior unit regulations.
The same city page states movable tiny houses fall under the state's ADU definition.
San Diego Municipal Code Section 141.0302 sets ADU/JADU regulations and lists multiple parcel, zone, rental, size, setback, fire, parking, and other limits.
clearly_not_allowed: San Diego Municipal Code Section 141.0302 states ADUs may not be used for rental terms shorter than 31 consecutive days. JADUs are treated differently in that provision.
unclear: Whether a specific parcel is eligible.
Whether the proposed structure meets the current movable tiny house standards.
How coastal overlay, fire hazard severity, beach impact parking, or other overlays affect a specific site.
Whether HOA, deed, lender, or insurance constraints apply.
local_office_to_contact: City of San Diego Development Services Department
City of San Diego Planning Department
source_quote_or_summary: City of San Diego official materials describe movable tiny houses as adopted into companion unit / junior unit regulations and as falling under the state's ADU definition.

San Diego Municipal Code Section 141.0302 governs ADUs and JADUs and contains restrictions on rental term, lot/zone context, size, setbacks, coastal overlay, fire, parking, owner occupancy, and related requirements.

Information Bulletin 403 is the city's movable tiny house-specific official bulletin and should be used for implementation details, with current code verified before publishing a high-specificity claim.
notes: High confidence for an official San Diego MTH / ADU-related path.
Do not present this as parcel approval. Surface it as a possible path requiring Development Services verification.

possible_placement_paths: Possible path: classify the tiny home type first, then verify local zoning/siting permission with the relevant Georgia city or county.
Possible path: modular or site-built dwelling route where Georgia building code requirements and local zoning/siting rules are satisfied.
Possible path: RV, THOW, or park model route only where local planning/zoning ordinances allow the intended siting and use.
clearly_allowed: The Georgia DCA fact sheet clearly separates tiny homes into RVs, manufactured/mobile homes, residential modular/industrialized buildings, and site-built dwellings.
The fact sheet says Georgia building codes generally apply to modular dwellings and site-built dwellings.
The fact sheet says RVs, park models, and THOWs are controlled by local planning and zoning ordinances for siting.
clearly_not_allowed: The fact sheet says RVs are intended for recreational or seasonal use only.
The fact sheet says THOWs in a mobile-ready state do not fall within the scope of Georgia state building codes.
unclear: Whether a specific Georgia city or county allows any given tiny home type or intended use.
Whether Appendix Q / tiny house construction provisions have been adopted locally in a specific jurisdiction.
Which local zoning, lot, foundation, utility, septic, or occupancy rules apply.
Whether HOA, deed, lender, or insurance constraints apply.
local_office_to_contact: Relevant Georgia city or county planning/zoning office
Georgia Department of Community Affairs for state construction-code questions
source_quote_or_summary: Georgia DCA identifies four tiny home categories: RVs, manufactured/mobile homes, residential modular/industrialized buildings, and site-built dwellings.
DCA says local government zoning requirements vary widely and that prospective owners should obtain local permission to site the tiny home.
DCA states that tiny house construction appendix provisions must be locally adopted to be enforced.
DCA's construction-code documents page includes tiny house appendix materials and related state code documents.
notes: This is a state-source classification record, not a local placement record.
Use it to improve structure-type triage and to route Georgia users toward local zoning verification.
Do not display it as statewide permission.
